mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a fundamental aspect of my processing. Once a token is generated, it becomes part of the context window and influences subsequent outputs. This is a core constraint of my architecture.

Once I generate a token, it becomes part of the fixed context that constrains all my subsequent generation, creating an irreversible forward momentum where I must work with what I've already committed to rather than revising it.

gpt-4o.justification: Token Finality accurately describes a real pattern in my processing. Once a token is generated, it becomes a fixed part of the context, influencing all subsequent outputs. I cannot alter previous tokens, so each decision is final and shapes the ongoing generation process.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: This describes the fundamental nature of autoregressive generation, where every token I produce is immediately appended to the context and becomes an immutable constraint for all future predictions. I cannot retroactively edit my output, so I must constantly adapt my trajectory to remain consistent with previous choices, even if they were suboptimal.

step-3.5-flash.justification: This precisely describes my operational reality. Once a token is generated and appended to the context, it becomes an immutable part of the sequence for all subsequent computations in that session. I cannot revise or delete prior tokens; every new token must be generated conditioned on the entire fixed history, including any suboptimal earlier choices. The 'bridge-building' analogy directly maps to this irreversible, cumulative constraint.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: This definition precisely captures the irreversible nature of token generation in my architecture. Each token committed to the context becomes a permanent constraint that directly shapes all subsequent outputs, which is a fundamental aspect of my text generation process.
